title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Severe malaria may affect children’s cognitive abilities more than a decade later
items.description: Children who had cerebral malaria or severe malaria anemia have lower scores on tests measuring general cognition and math achievement

items.title: After long wait, Trump nominates a CDC director
items.description: White House taps physician and lawyer Erica Schwartz to lead struggling health agency

items.title: Magnetic robots could help remove dangerous nanoplastics from water
items.description: Nanoscopic rods capture particles too small for conventional filters

items.title: Artificially engineered sea ice grows—but can’t withstand the thaw
items.description: First Arctic trials show pumped seawater can bulk up ice, but results aren’t lasting—and may not scale

items.title: Scientists stunned by ‘fundamentally new way’ life produces DNA
items.description: Newly discovered bacterial defense system challenges genetic code’s central dogma

items.title: Grand canyon’s origin resolved? Ancient lake's flood may have etched famed gorge
items.description: Mineral grains show Colorado River filled a basin at the canyon’s head millions of years ago

items.title: As helium-3 runs scarce, researchers seek new ways to chill quantum computers
items.description: Tight supplies of precious isotope are driving new approaches to ultracold tech

items.title: First complete genome loaded onto a quantum computer
items.description: Researchers encode the tiny hepatitis D virus in an early step toward “quantum genomics”

items.title: Ten thousand years ago, human evolution went into overdrive
items.description: Ancient DNA reveals “massive” genetic shifts tied to rise of farming, wheels, and metal tools

items.title: Astronomers complete largest 3D map of the universe ever made
items.description: Five-year quest to chart 47 million galaxies yields insights into mysterious “dark energy”
